features.name: Phase 0 - Stabilize Harness And Baseline
features.description: Keep the coding harness reliable and resolve or explicitly isolate the current OpenClaw SDK typecheck blocker before gateway implementation expands.
codebaseMemoryQueries: Find imports of openclaw/plugin-sdk/zalouser and usage of OpenClawConfig MarkdownTableMode RuntimeEnv
Trace src/channel/monitor.ts callers before adding compatibility boundary
doneCriteria: Harness validator score remains at least 85/100.
npm test passes after harness updates.
The typecheck blocker is either fixed or explicitly documented as the only reason ./init.sh is blocked.
docs/GATEWAY_PLAN.md is present and referenced by harness state files.

verificationEvidence.notes: 9 test files passed, 104 tests passed before plan update. Rerun after latest plan edits.
Fails at npm run typecheck because openclaw/plugin-sdk/zalouser does not export OpenClawConfig, MarkdownTableMode, or RuntimeEnv used by src/channel/monitor.ts.

features.evidence: Gateway implementation plan is being added in docs/GATEWAY_PLAN.md.

features.name: Phase 1 - Standalone Gateway Skeleton
features.description: Add a standalone HTTP server entrypoint and health/version routes without requiring OpenClaw runtime or real Zalo login in tests.
codebaseMemoryQueries: Inspect current package scripts and module format before adding standalone entrypoint
Find existing config parsing conventions in src/config
doneCriteria: Server can be instantiated in tests without real Zalo login.
GET /health returns service status and Zalo client connection state.
GET /version returns package/version/runtime info.
Auth helper and route shape have tests.

features.name: Phase 2 - Zalo Client Boundary
features.description: Wrap zca-js login/session/listener/send behavior behind a gateway-owned interface so route code does not depend on OpenClaw runtime.
codebaseMemoryQueries: Trace send message path from channel/tool to zca-js
Find login and QR session lifecycle in client modules
doneCriteria: Gateway route code depends only on GatewayZaloClient interface.
Unit tests use a mocked Zalo client.
Existing OpenClaw plugin code remains test-compatible.

features.name: Phase 3 - Messaging MVP
features.description: Add authenticated POST /messages/send and normalized inbound message webhook dispatch.
codebaseMemoryQueries: Find existing message normalization and dedup logic
Find existing webhook or queue patterns if any
doneCriteria: POST /messages/send requires bearer token.
Route validates required fields and returns stable JSON errors.
Inbound event shape is normalized and documented.
Webhook dispatcher handles success and failure paths in tests.

features.name: Phase 4 - Curated Action Registry
features.description: Expose POST /actions/:action for a small stable subset of zaloclaw actions before expanding toward the full action surface.
codebaseMemoryQueries: Inspect ZaloClawToolSchema and executeZaloClawTool action dispatch
Find reusable validation for send reaction thread info group members mark read
doneCriteria: Action registry has a typed handler map.
Initial actions include send, reply-message, add-reaction, get-thread-info, get-group-members, and mark-read where supported.
Tests cover each initial action with mocked Zalo client.
Docs list supported actions and phased action coverage.

features.name: Phase 5 - Hermes Integration Adapter
features.description: Connect Hermes to the gateway through a thin external bridge or plugin without modifying Hermes core.
codebaseMemoryQueries: Inspect Hermes gateway plugin constraints only after standalone gateway is stable
doneCriteria: A Zalo message can trigger Hermes through webhook or adapter in a documented smoke flow.
Hermes reply can be sent back through POST /messages/send.
No Hermes core files are modified.
